definition: Magics are special IPython commands prefixed with % (line magic) or %% (cell magic) that add notebook superpowers like timing, profiling, environment control and package installs; keyboard shortcuts drive cell editing and execution without the mouse.
layman: Magics are handy built-in shortcuts you type into cells (like a stopwatch or installer), and keyboard shortcuts let you fly through editing and running cells.
content:
## Magics & shortcuts

### Line vs cell magics

- **Line magic** `%name` acts on the rest of that line: `%timeit f()`, `%env KEY`.
- **Cell magic** `%%name` acts on the whole cell: `%%time`, `%%writefile out.py`.

Useful magics:

| Magic | Does |
|-------|------|
| `%time` / `%timeit` | time one run / average many runs |
| `%%time` / `%%timeit` | time a whole cell |
| `%who` / `%whos` | list variables in the kernel |
| `%pip install x` | install into the kernel's own environment |
| `%env KEY=value` | view/set environment variables |
| `%load_ext autoreload` + `%autoreload 2` | auto-reimport edited modules |
| `%run script.py` | execute a script in the kernel |

### Shell commands with !

`!command` runs a shell command (e.g. `!ls`, `!git status`). For installing packages prefer **`%pip`** over `!pip`: `%pip` targets the *kernel's* environment, avoiding the classic "installed but can't import" mismatch.

### Keyboard shortcuts (modes)

Notebooks have two modes:
- **Edit mode** (Enter): type inside a cell.
- **Command mode** (Esc): act on cells.

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Shift+Enter | run cell, select next |
| Ctrl/Cmd+Enter | run cell in place |
| Alt/Opt+Enter | run cell, insert below |
| (cmd) a / b | insert cell above / below |
| (cmd) dd | delete cell |
| (cmd) m / y | to Markdown / to code |
| (cmd) z | undo cell operation |

### Why this matters for Gen AI

You'll `%pip install` SDKs into the right kernel, `%env` an API key for a session, `%timeit` an embedding or token-counting routine, `%%time` a model call to spot latency, and use autoreload while editing helper modules — all without leaving the notebook.
keyPoints: % is line magic (one line); %% is cell magic (whole cell).
Prefer %pip over !pip so installs land in the kernel's environment.
Edit mode (Enter) types; command mode (Esc) acts on cells.
Shift+Enter runs and advances; a/b/dd/m/y manage cells in command mode.
